title: Mag-Bind® Blood & Tissue DNA HDQ 96 Kit
author: Opentrons (verified)
materials: Mag-Bind® Blood & Tissue DNA HDQ 96 Kit

steps.instruction: Your OT-2 can fully automate the entire Mag-Bind® Blood & Tissue DNA HDQ 96 Kit. Results of the Opentrons Science team's internal testing of this protocol on the OT-2 are shown below: Explanation of complex parameters below: * `Number of samples`: Specify the number of samples this run (1-96 and divisible by 8, i.e. whole columns at a time). * `Deepwell type`: Specify which well plate will be mounted on the magnetic module. * `Reservoir Type`: Specify which reservoir will be employed. * `Starting Volume`: Specify starting volume of sample (ul). * `Binding Buffer Volume`: Specify the volume of binding buffer to use (ul). * `Elution Volume`: Specify elution volume (ul). * `Park Tips`: Specify whether to park tips or drop tips. * `P300 Multi Channel Pipette Mount`: Specify whether the P300 multi channel pipette will be on the left or right mount. ---
Binding buffer is added to samples on deep well plate on the magnetic module.
Magnetic module is engaged and incubated for 7 minutes.
Supernatant is removed and dropped into the liquid waste container.
Add Wash Buffer and resuspend by mixing.
Remove supernatant to liquid waste container.
Perform second wash (repeats step 5-6 with second wash buffer set).
Perform third wash (repeats step 5-6 with third wash buffer without resuspension).
Incubate for 1 minute to dry beads.
Elution solution is added to the samples.
Beads are resuspended in elution solution.
Magnetic module is engaged and incubates for 7 minutes (variable settling time).
Elution samples are transferred to elution plate in Slot 1.
